[Ax01]
  • Get Started
  • Docs
  • Axicons
GitHub

Alert

Semantic alert component for displaying important messages. Single `.alert` class with `data-variant` for status types.

Alert Types

Success Alert

Success! Your action completed successfully.

Error Alert

Error! Something went wrong. Please try again.

Warning Alert

Warning: This action cannot be undone.

Info Alert

Information: New features are now available. 
<div class="alert" data-variant="success" role="alert">
    <span class="axicon render" data-name="CheckCircle"></span>
    <div>
        <strong>Success!</strong> Your action completed.
    </div>
</div>

<div class="alert" data-variant="error" role="alert">
    <span class="axicon render" data-name="AlertCircle"></span>
    <div>
        <strong>Error!</strong> Something went wrong.
    </div>
</div>

<div class="alert" data-variant="warning" role="alert">
    <span class="axicon render" data-name="AlertTriangle"></span>
    <div>
        <strong>Warning:</strong> Important notice.
    </div>
</div>

<div class="alert" data-variant="info" role="alert">
    <span class="axicon render" data-name="Info"></span>
    <div>
        <strong>Information:</strong> New features available.
    </div>
</div>

Markup

Alert uses semantic HTML with single `.alert` class:

<div class="alert" data-variant="success" role="alert">
    <span class="axicon render" data-name="IconName"></span>
    <div>
        <strong>Title</strong> Message text...
    </div>
</div>

Variants

Use `data-variant` attribute for different alert types:

  • `data-variant="success"` - Success/confirmation message (green)
  • `data-variant="error"` - Error message (red)
  • `data-variant="warning"` - Warning message (orange)
  • `data-variant="info"` - Informational message (blue)

Key Features

  • Semantic Structure - Single `.alert` class with semantic divs
  • Data Variants - `data-variant` for different types
  • Axicons - Semantic icons for visual clarity
  • ARIA Attributes - `role="alert"` for accessibility
  • Icon + Text - Never rely on color alone
  • Design Tokens - Colors from token system

Accessibility

  • `role="alert"` - Announces to screen readers immediately
  • Icons paired with text - Never color only
  • Clear, descriptive text - Users understand the message
  • Sufficient contrast - Meets WCAG AA standards
  • Readable text size - Minimum 16px on mobile
  • Keyboard accessible - Can receive focus if interactive

Responsive Behavior

Alert components automatically adapt to mobile and desktop screens:

Mobile (< 768px)

On mobile devices, alerts display optimally within constrained width:

  • Full-width alerts with padding maintained
  • Icon stacks on mobile if needed
  • Close button clearly visible and tappable (44px+ target)
  • Text wraps naturally without overflow

Desktop (1024px+)

Desktop displays use full width with enhanced styling:

  • Full-width within container
  • Icon and text side-by-side
  • Smooth transitions on hover
  • Close button easily accessible

Framework Integration

Alerts are semantic components with automatic responsiveness:

  • No grid required - alerts respond to container width
  • Use within `.container` or `.grid` for layout
  • Padding and gaps scale with viewport automatically
  • All sizes maintain WCAG AAA color contrast

Best Practices

  • Use appropriate variant for message type
  • Keep messages concise and actionable
  • Include descriptive icon for visual clarity
  • Use strong/bold for title when appropriate
  • Place alerts where users will see them
  • Use icon + text, never color alone
  • Test with keyboard navigation
  • Use `role="alert"` for dynamic alerts
[Ax01]
